zh.lifeSummary: Lord Robert Cheney IV Earl是Sir Robert Cheney Sr.的兒子，於1510年6月29日出生在英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比。他和Joane Harrison至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1569年11月26日在英國米德萊可倫敦 比曉普斯蓋特享年59歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 波托爾福 維特 比曉普斯蓋特。

nameMeanings.name: Cheney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Chaney   with the same etymology as in 2 below.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by or in an oak wood, from Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’ (from chesne ‘oak’), or a habitational name from any of several places called (Le) Cheney, e.g. in Haute-Savoie, named with this word. Compare Chesney .

French Canadian: altered form of French Chesnay or Chenay, topographic names with the same meaning and etymology as in 2 above, or habitational names from (Le) Chesnay or (Le) Chenay, names of several places in the northwestern part of France, based on Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’. Compare Chenette and Chenier 2.

variants.name: Chenette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Armand.
Altered form of French Canadian Chenet, itself an (intermediate) altered form of Cheney 3. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.
History: The (majority of the) Chenettes are descendants of Bertrand Chesnay/Chenay dit/sieur de La Garenne from Brittany (France); see Cheney .

variants.name: Chaney
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any one of several places in Normandy, Picardy, or Artois (France) named (Le) Quesnay or Quesnoy, or elsewhere in northern France named (Le or La) Chesnay or Chesnoy. The placenames all derive from Old French chesnai or its Normanno-Picard equivalent quesnai ‘oak grove’ (medieval Latin casnetum).
French: variant of Chanet, a habitational name from any of various places called Chanet or Le Chanet, from Latin canna ‘reed’ + the suffix -etum denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Lord Robert Cheney IV Earl was born on 29 June 1510, in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England as the son of Sir Robert Cheney Sr.. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Joane Harrison. He died on 26 November 1569, in London Bishopsgate, Middlesex, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Saint Botolph without Bishopsgate, London, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Robert Cheney IV Earlは1510年6月29日にイギリスエセックスワルサム アビーでSir Robert Cheney Sr.の息子として生まれた。彼はJoane Harrisonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1569年11月26日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドン ビショプスガトで59歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンサイント ボトルフ ウィソウト ビショプスガトにて埋葬された。

ko.St Botolph without Bishopsgate, Bishopsg: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 세인트 벗프 위토우트 비숍스가트
ko.lifeSummary: Lord Robert Cheney IV Earl(은)는 1510년 6월 29일 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에서 Sir Robert Cheney Sr.의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Joane Harrison(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1569년 11월 26일 영국 미들레세스 런던 비숍스가트에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 세인트 벗프 위토우트 비숍스가트에 묻혔다.

ru.St Botolph without Bishopsgate, Bishopsg: сеинт ботолф витоут бишопсгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Lord Robert Cheney IV Earl родился 29 июня 1510 г. в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия. Его отца звали Sir Robert Cheney Sr. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Joane Harrison. Он умер 26 ноября 1569 г. в местности лондон бишопсгэит, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт ботолф витоут бишопсгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
